title: "THE STARVING OF THE SEEDLINGS: How Aotearoa's Coalition of Cruelty Chose Corporate Efficiency Over the Hunger of Papatūānuku's Tamariki" - 9 March 2026v
tags: over 40 million students;**Koha — Support the Māori Green Lantern**;**Subscribe to the Māori Green Lantern**;more than 220,000 students;Beehive announced;$8.68 under Labour to $3.46 under Seymour;some schools began paying out of their own budgets to feed children;Compass Group was excluded;Cabinet agreed to rename the programme;35.1% of Māori children;23.5% of Indigenous Māori students;9.5% miss meals four or more times a week.;169,300 children in Aotearoa live in material hardship;Māori and Pacific children are hardest hit;The Child Poverty Action Group;third highest in the OECD;the PNAE serves over 40 million students;at least 30% of the food budget must be sourced from local family farmers;promotes food and nutrition education, respects regional culture, and supports short supply chains;As the Spinoff documented;New Zealand has the third highest rate in the OECD for students missing meals;The _Ka Ora, Ka Ako_ programme had already demonstrated measurable impacts;RNZ;Cabinet-approved on 20 October 2024;iwi/hapū food providers;has since been excluded from the programme;Cabinet's OIA-released papers;$130 million per year;As confirmed by RNZ's Budget 2025 coverage;The CPAG's formal budget bid;Child Poverty Action Group;Dr Pippa McKelvie-Sebileau's research team;_Te Kūkupa Kore Parirau_
